title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter after allegedly stabbing boyfriend Kyle Bradford, 26, in South Boston.
Pascual claims self-defense, citing threats from Bradford; police found her with injuries and Bradford unarmed.
Pascual held on $100,000 bond; Bradford had multiple dismissed cases including domestic assault in Massachusetts, Georgia.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will appear on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the first time in 2027.
'Hard Knocks' will feature the Seattle Seahawks in 2026 after their Super Bowl win against the Patriots.
Changes in NFL rules made the Patriots eligible despite past objections during Belichick's tenure.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a top Boston Bruins prospect, scored his first pro goal and assist with the Providence Bruins this weekend.
Hagens contributed to Providence's 6-4 win over the Bridgeport Islanders and 4-2 loss to the Springfield Thunderbirds.
Currently on an AHL amateur tryout contract, Hagens may join the Bruins if he signs an entry-level contract.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to a $26.8 million cap hit but remain open to re-signing him.
Eliot Wolf stated that the Patriots might consider bringing back Diggs if team needs change.
Diggs recorded 1,013 yards and four touchdowns in 2025, but struggled in the postseason.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was sentenced to at least 50 years in prison for the 1989 rape of two women.
Gale was convicted on four counts of aggravated rape, two counts of kidnapping, and one count of armed robbery.
DNA and investigative genetic genealogy led to Gale's identification and conviction more than 30 years after the crimes.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla dismisses NBA Coach of the Year award as 'stupid,' emphasizing team and staff effort over individual accolades.
The Celtics have achieved a 50-win season, are second in the Eastern Conference, despite Jayson Tatum's absence.
Mazzulla stresses the importance of player development and team support over personal coaching achievements.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person died in a car crash on American Legion Highway in Mattapan late Sunday night, according to police.
The crash, involving extensive car damage, occurred at 11:14 p.m. near Walk Hill and Canterbury Streets.
Boston Police found the victim deceased at the scene with the vehicle on the sidewalk.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds to win NBA Coach of the Year, following Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
The Celtics, led by Mazzulla, are second in the Eastern Conference despite losing key players during the offseason.
Mazzulla's development focus improved player performance, turning weaknesses into strengths while maintaining a top-four league ranking both offensively and defensively.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An 'END ICE' ice sculpture on Boston Common was destroyed by vandals after the 'No Kings' rally.
The sculpture by artist Kat Dressler was designed to carry its message even if vandalized.
Boston police responded to the incident, but no report was filed as the organizer wasn't on site.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, from Worcester, sentenced to 30 years for sexually exploiting minors, including a 6-year-old girl.
Merced pleaded guilty to sexual exploitation and possession of child pornography; his sentence is concurrent with existing state sentence.
Merced was arrested in September 2024; federal investigation found he abused three children and created abuse videos.
